title: Unable to map uids or gids for container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I'm trying to map my host user to the user that the mongo image runs the database as, but keep getting:
```
Error: Container ID 0 cannot be mapped to a host ID
```

The goal is to fix permissions for database files, so that my user on the host can see the database files as if they were created locally.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `mkdir db`

2. `podman run -it --rm -v ./db:/data/db:z --uidmap 999:$(id -u):1 mongo:3`

**Describe the results you received:**

`Error: Container ID 0 cannot be mapped to a host ID`

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected the host user to be mapped to the mongodb user (999 in the image), thus creating the files in `./db` as if they were created by the host user. I'm surprised it's failing with `ID 0`, since I specified 999 as the container ID.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

`podman run -it --rm -v ./db:/data/db:z --uidmap 0:$(id -u):999 mongo:3` doesn't produce the same error, but gives:
```
chown: changing ownership of '/data/configdb': Invalid argument
chown: changing ownership of '/data/db': Invalid argument
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.14.9
Built:        Wed Sep 30 15:31:11 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.16.1
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.21-2.fc32.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.21, commit: 81d18b6c3ffc266abdef7ca94c1450e669a6a388'
  cpus: 4
  distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "32"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: yoga
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.8.15-201.fc32.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 993521664
  memTotal: 8188657664
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.15-5.fc32.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.15
      commit: 56ca95e61639510c7dbd39ff512f80f626404969
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.1.4-1.fc32.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.4
      commit: b66ffa8e262507e37fca689822d23430f3357fe8
      libslirp: 4.3.1
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 2
  swapFree: 8191471616
  swapTotal: 8191471616
  uptime: 2h 11m 51.68s (Approximately 0.08 days)
registries:
  search:
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
  - docker.io
store:
  configFile: /home/zeranoe/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 2
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 2
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: fuse-overlayfs-1.2.0-1.fc32.x86_64
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.9.1
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.1.0
        FUSE library version 3.9.1
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
  graphRoot: /home/zeranoe/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 4
  runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers
  volumePath: /home/zeranoe/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 2.0.0
  Built: 1601494271
  BuiltTime: Wed Sep 30 15:31:11 2020
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.14.9
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 2.1.1

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.1.1-7.fc32.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

I get the same error with `--gidmap`, and the docs seem to indicate that these two commands do the same exact thing.

>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8129>, or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCH7SUPAKOKP3TQ2H33SMNINZANCNFSM4S54KPCQ>
> .
>

the command: `podman run -it --rm -v ./db:/data/db:z --uidmap 999:$(id -u):1 mongo:3` is mapping a single UID in the user namespace.  There is no root user available inside the user namespace.

Can you make sure there is a mapping also for root?

It looks like the command I actually want is: `podman run -it --rm -v ./db:/data/db:z --uidmap=0:1:1 --uidmap=999:0:1 mongo:3`

https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/6123 helped a lot, but this is still pretty confusing in my opinion.

> --uidmap=0:1:1 --uidmap=999:0:1

I came across this issue a few months back and followed this to resolve my issue. Bumping to podman 3.2.3 (from 2.2.1) broke this solution for me. Removing the `--uidmap 0:1:1` alongside ensuring a default `containers.conf` was created (see [here](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1882136#c5)) got me back to running.
